nameMeanings.name: Emma
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of compound names such as Ermintrude containing the word erm(en), irm(en) ‘entire’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, but its popularity in medieval England was greatly enhanced by the fact that it had been borne by the mother of Edward the Confessor, herself a Norman. In modern times, it was only in moderate use early in the 20th century but rose sharply in favour in the 1970s and has since remained perennially popular.

en.lifeSummary: When Emma Soligny was born in 1169, in Umberleigh, Devon, England, United Kingdom, her father, Hasculf II de Soligny, Seigneur de Dol, was 25 and her mother, Isolda Iseult Dol, was 30. She died in 1266, in her hometown, at the age of 97.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Emma Soligny est née en 1169, à Umberleigh, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Hasculf II de Soligny, Seigneur de Dol, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Isolda Iseult Dol, avait 30 ans. Elle est décédée en 1266, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 97 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Emma Soligny(이)가 1169년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 벌리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hasculf II de Soligny, Seigneur de Dol(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isolda Iseult Dol(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1266년 그녀의 고향에서 97세의 나이로 사망했다.
